| Elo min date          = late 2009

| First game            = '''Unofficial'''<br>{{flagdeco|Georgian SSR|1922}} '''Georgian SSR''' 2–4  [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan SSR]] {{flagicon image|Flag of the Azerbaijan Soviet Socialist Republic (1924–1927).svg}}<br>([[Tbilisi]], [[Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic|Georgian SSR]]; December 1926)<br>'''Official'''<br>{{fb|LTU|1988}} 1–0 {{fb-rt|GEO|1990}}<br>([[Kaunas]], [[Lithuania]]; 2 September 1992)
| Largest win           = {{fb|GEO|2023}} 8–0 {{fb-rt|THA}}<br>([[Tbilisi]], Georgia; 12 October 2023)
| Largest loss          = {{fb|GEO}} 1–7 {{fb-rt|ESP}}<br>([[Tbilisi]], Georgia; 8 September 2023)
| World cup apps        = 
| World cup first       = 
| World cup best        = 
| Regional name      = [[UEFA European Championship|European Championship]]
| Regional cup apps  = 1
| Regional cup first = [[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]]
| Regional cup best  = Round of 16 ([[UEFA Euro 2024|2024]])
|website={{URL|https://nakrebi.ge/ka|nakrebi.ge}} {{in lang|ka}}
}}

The '''Georgia national football team''' ({{lang-ka|საქართველოს ეროვნული საფეხბურთო ნაკრები|tr}}) represents [[Georgia (country)|Georgia]] in men's international [[association football|football]] matches, and is controlled by the [[Georgian Football Federation]], the governing body for [[Football in Georgia (country)|football in Georgia]]. It is a member of [[UEFA]] in Europe and [[FIFA]] in global competitions. 

The Georgian team's first match took place on 27 May 1990 against [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]], while Georgia was still part of the [[Soviet Union]]. The team have attempted to qualify for each major tournament from [[1996 UEFA European Football Championship|Euro 1996]] onwards. Their efforts culminated in their first successful qualification for a major tournament, the [[UEFA Euro 2024]], secured with a win against [[Greece national football team|Greece]] in the [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying play-offs|UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying play-off final]].

Along with [[Russia national football team|Russia]], [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]] and [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]], Georgia is one of the few post-Soviet states to qualify for the [[UEFA European Championship]] finals, though it has yet to take part in a [[FIFA World Cup]].

The team is colloquially referred to as Jvarosnebi ([[Crusades|The Crusaders]]) and has adopted [[Saint George]] as their symbol, which is a [[Patron saint|patron]] of Georgia.

Upon its admission into FIFA ranked 156th, Georgia ascended to their peak [[FIFA Men's World Ranking|World Ranking]] of 42nd in 1998. The team plays their home games at the [[Boris Paichadze Dinamo Arena]] in [[Tbilisi]].

==History==
=== 20th century ===
Football was introduced to Georgia by [[English people|English]] sailors in early 20th century. [[British Empire|British]] ships that docked at the harbors of [[Poti]] and [[Batumi]] frequently hosted football matches among their crew, attracting the attention of [[Lelo burti]] players, who quickly embraced and adapted the sport. While the exact date of the first football match in Georgia remains uncertain, football was first mentioned in the Georgian press in 1890.<ref>{{Cite web |title=სპორტის სახეობის აღწერა |url=http://sport.gov.ge/?pg=sporttypes&sportid=51 |access-date=2024-10-16 |website=sport.gov.ge}}</ref>

From 1923 to 1990, Georgian football players were part of the [[Soviet Union national football team|USSR national team]], with two of them, [[Murtaz Khurtsilava]] and [[Aleksandre Chivadze]], serving as captains at different times.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Erik |first=Scott |title=Soccer Artistry and the Secret Police: Georgian Football in the Multiethnic Soviet Empire |url=https://www.idfi.ge/archive/index.php?cat=read_topic&topic=151&lang=en |website=IDFI}}</ref> During the early Soviet era, Georgia competed in the [[Transcaucasian Socialist Federative Soviet Republic|Transcaucasian]] Championship, hosting the 1926 edition. Georgia's first game came against [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]] in Tbilisi, losing 4–2 in [[Tbilisi]]. The Georgian SSR's first win came in their second game, beating [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] 7–0. Georgia would later host the 1927 and 1928 editions of the Transcaucasian Championship, winning the latter edition after a 5–1 and 6–0 win over Armenia and Azerbaijan respectively. After a six-year hiatus, Georgia played in the 1934 competition in Azerbaijan, before once against hosting the 1935 Transcaucasian Championship, winning it once again.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.eloratings.net/Georgia|title=Georgia matches, ratings and points exchanged|publisher=ELO Ratings|access-date=7 July 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.international-football.net/competition-overview?compet-id=Trans-Caucasian%20Championship|title=Trans-Caucasian Championship|publisher=International Football|access-date=7 July 2024}}</ref>

During this period, the USSR national team included players from various [[Republics of the Soviet Union|Soviet republics]], including [[Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic|Georgian SSR]]. Georgian players made significant contributions to the team, with several becoming key figures and achieving notable success, such as [[Slava Metreveli]] scoring in the [[1960 European Nations' Cup final]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=History: USSR 2-1 Yugoslavia |url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/match/4025--ussr-vs-yugoslavia/ |website=UEFA}}</ref> One of the most famous Georgian footballers who played for the USSR national team was Murtaz Khurtsilava, who captained the team and played a crucial role in the [[1966 FIFA World Cup]], where the USSR finished in fourth place. Another prominent player was [[Vitaly Daraselia]], known for his performances in the 1980s, along with [[David Kipiani]], [[Ramaz Shengelia]] and [[Vladimir Gutsaev]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Khashig |first=Inal |date=February 10, 2017 |title=15 soccer players that the Caucasus is proud of |url=https://jam-news.net/15-soccer-players-that-the-caucasus-is-proud-of/ |website=JAMnews}}</ref>
[[File:KinkGeo.jpg|left|thumb|155px|[[Georgi Kinkladze]], ''the Georgian Maradona'', made his debut in 1992.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2015/sep/17/golden-goal-georgi-kinkladze-southampton|title=Golden Goal: Georgi Kinkladze for Manchester City v Southampton (1996)|work=The Guardian|last=Nakrani|first=Sachin|date=17 September 2015|accessdate=20 June 2018}}</ref>]]
<br />
Following Soviet rule in Georgia, the contemporary history of the Georgia national football team began in 1990, when the team played their first international match against [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]], the first country to accept an invitation. The match was held on 27 May 1990 at the [[Boris Paichadze Dinamo Arena|national stadium]].<ref name="RSSSF" /> Georgia were coached by [[Givi Nodia]]. The friendly match ended in a 2–2 draw. This was the only match prior to the declaration of [[Independence Day (Georgia)|independence]] on 9 April 1991. Soon afterwards the team played another friendly match against [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]].

The [[Georgian Football Federation]] became a member of both [[UEFA]] and [[FIFA]] in 1992,<ref>{{cite web |title=Georgian Football Federation |work=UEFA |url=http://www.uefa.com/memberassociations/association=geo/index.html |access-date=30 December 2006 |archive-date=12 April 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100412003727/http://www.uefa.com/memberassociations/association=geo/index.html |url-status=dead }}</ref> enabling Georgia to play competitive matches. The first of these came in September 1994, a 1–0 defeat to [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]]<ref name="RSSSF">{{cite web |title=Georgia – International Results |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/geor-intres.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091015054743/http://www.rsssf.com/tablesg/geor-intres.html |archive-date=15 October 2009 |access-date=30 December 2006 |work=RSSSF}}</ref> as part of the [[UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying Group 7|qualifiers]] for [[UEFA Euro 1996|Euro 1996]]. Georgia finished third in their group, ahead of Moldova, [[Wales national football team|Wales]] and [[Albania national football team|Albania]], but seven points behind second-placed qualifier [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]].

Georgia failed to [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 2|qualify]] for the [[1998 World Cup]] in France, obtaining 10 points and finishing in fourth place, level on points with [[Poland national football team|Poland]]. At this time, Georgia reached 42nd place in the [[FIFA World Ranking]].

=== 21st century ===
[[File:Kakhaber Kaladze.jpg|right|thumb|220px|[[Kakha Kaladze]], former captain of the national team.]]
During [[UEFA Euro 2000]] [[UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying Group 2|qualifying]] the Georgia national team won one match, drew two and finished at the bottom of the group with five points.

The team finished fifth (and last) with seven points in their qualifying [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying Group 10|group]] for [[Euro 2004]], although they defeated [[Russia national football team|Russia]] with a goal scored by [[Malkhaz Asatiani]].

In the [[2006 World Cup]] [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification - UEFA Group 2|qualifiers]] Georgia beat [[Albania national football team|Albania]] 2–0 at home and [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] away 2–1. They finished sixth  of seven with ten points in Group 2.

Georgia were sixth out of seven teams in the [[Euro 2008]] [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying Group B|qualifying group]] with ten points. They defeated [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]] 2–0 at home and the [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]] 6–0 away and 3–1 at home.

[[Héctor Cúper]] became the manager of Georgia in August 2008. During the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 8|qualification]] round for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]] Georgia failed to win any matches and finished sixth (and last) with three points.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Wilson |first=Jonathan |date=2009-02-10 |title=Strict Cuper remains serious about beleaguered Georgia's hopes |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/blog/2009/feb/10/jonathan-wilson-hector-cuper-georgia-ireland |access-date=2024-01-12 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=12 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112161410/https://www.theguardian.com/football/blog/2009/feb/10/jonathan-wilson-hector-cuper-georgia-ireland |url-status=live }}</ref> Cuper didn't extend his contract, and on 6 November 2009, [[Temur Ketsbaia]] was appointed as the new manager of the Georgian national team. Ketsbaia resigned as manager after a 4–0 defeat at home to Poland in the [[Euro 2016]] [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group D|qualifiers]] on 14 November 2014, having previously stated he would do so before the match regardless of the result.<ref>{{Cite web |title=European Qualifiers: Temuri Ketsbaia quits as coach of Georgia |url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/19692/9564990/georgia-boss-ketsbaia-steps-down |access-date=2024-01-12 |website=Sky Sports |language=en |archive-date=12 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112161408/https://www.skysports.com/football/news/19692/9564990/georgia-boss-ketsbaia-steps-down |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-11-15 |title=Temuri Ketsbaia quits as Georgia manager |url=https://www.espn.co.uk/football/story/_/id/37390746/temuri-ketsbaia-quits-georgia-manager |access-date=2024-01-12 |website=ESPN.com |language=en |archive-date=12 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112161409/https://www.espn.co.uk/football/story/_/id/37390746/temuri-ketsbaia-quits-georgia-manager |url-status=live }}</ref>

[[File:TUR-GEO_18.06.24,_Batum_(4).jpg|left|thumb|Georgian fans in the Batumi fan zone celebrating their first goal vs [[UEFA Euro 2024 Group F#Turkey vs Georgia|Turkey]] in [[Euro 2024]], their first goal in the history of the tournament.]]
In June 2016, Georgia beat the two-time reigning European champions [[Spain national football team|Spain]] 1–0 in their final pre-Euro 2016 friendly.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.espnfc.com/report?gameId=448090 |title=Spain fall to Georgia in final friendly ahead of Euro 2016 |publisher=[[ESPN FC]] |date=7 June 2016 |access-date=8 June 2016 |archive-date=8 June 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160608062133/http://www.espnfc.com/report?gameId=448090 |url-status=live }}</ref>

In 2018, they were the first team to earn promotion in the new [[UEFA Nations League]]. They scored the event's first goal in a [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D|UEFA Nations League D]] game in [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] before beating [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]] twice and [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]], with two games still to spare and earned their first-ever playoff berth.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nations League round-up: Ukraine, Georgia promoted as Gibraltar win again |url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/36166/11527505/nations-league-round-up-ukraine-georgia-promoted-as-gibraltar-win-again |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Sky Sports |language=en |archive-date=11 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111181845/https://www.skysports.com/football/news/36166/11527505/nations-league-round-up-ukraine-georgia-promoted-as-gibraltar-win-again |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-10-16 |title=Highlights: France beat Germany, Ukraine and Georgia celebrate {{!}} UEFA Nations League |url=https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/news/0253-0d8221c0a69c-f12aff803d41-1000--highlights-france-beat-germany-ukraine-and-georgia-celebrate/ |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=[[UEFA]] |language=en |archive-date=11 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111181845/https://www.uefa.com/uefanationsleague/news/0253-0d8221c0a69c-f12aff803d41-1000--highlights-france-beat-germany-ukraine-and-georgia-celebrate/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In the [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]], Georgia had a disappointing run, with their only two wins came over [[Gibraltar national football team|Gibraltar]]. In the Path D playoff semi-final, Georgia managed to beat [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]] 1–0 and thus the hope to qualify for [[UEFA Euro 2020]] increased,<ref>{{Cite web |agency=Associated Press |date=2020-10-08 |title=Georgia beats Belarus, advances to Euro 2020 playoff finals |url=https://www.sandiegouniontribune.com/sports/national/story/2020-10-08/georgia-beats-belarus-advances-to-euro-2020-playoff-finals |access-date=2024-01-12 |website=San Diego Union-Tribune |language=en |archive-date=12 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112161409/https://www.sandiegouniontribune.com/sports/national/story/2020-10-08/georgia-beats-belarus-advances-to-euro-2020-playoff-finals |url-status=live }}</ref> but it went in vain after the Georgians suffered a heartbreaking home defeat to [[North Macedonia national football team|North Macedonia]] in the decisive match and thus missed the opportunity to make a historic debut in a major competition.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-11-12 |title=Veteran Goran Pandev sends North Macedonia to Euro 2020 |url=https://www.eurosport.com/football/euro-qualifying/2020/veteran-pandev-sends-north-macedonia-to-euro-2020_sto7991819/story.shtml |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Eurosport |language=en |archive-date=24 June 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624091620/https://www.eurosport.com/football/euro-qualifying/2020/veteran-pandev-sends-north-macedonia-to-euro-2020_sto7991819/story.shtml |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=March 30, 2023 |title=Euro 2020: Playoff heartbreak for Georgia |url=https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/articles/euro-2020-playoff-heartbreak-for-georgia |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=www.fifa.com |url-status=live }}</ref>{{football squad on pitch|GK=[[Giorgi Mamardashvili|'''Mamardashvili''']]|LCB=[[Luka Lochoshvili|'''Lochoshvili''']]|RCB=[[Giorgi Gvelesiani|'''Gvelesiani''']]|RCM=[[Giorgi Chakvetadze|'''Chakvetadze''']]|LCM=[[Otar Kiteishvili|'''Kiteishvili''']]|caption=Starting lineup in Georgia's 2–0 win against Portugal at [[UEFA Euro 2024 Group F|UEFA Euro 2024]].|AM=[[Giorgi Kochorashvili|'''Kochorashvili''']]|clear=none|align=right|RB=[[Otar Kakabadze|'''Kakabadze''']]|LB=[[Lasha Dvali|'''Dvali''']]|ACB=[[Guram Kashia|'''Kashia (C)''']]|RCF=[[Georges Mikautadze|'''Mikautadze''']]|LCF=[[Khvicha Kvaratskhelia|'''Kvaratskhelia''']]}}Georgia showed improvements with a strong 2–0 win over [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]] at the [[2022 World Cup qualification]] on 11 November 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://sports.yahoo.com/sweden-shocker-georgia-opens-door-191008817.html|title=Sweden shocker in Georgia opens door for Spain|website=Yahoo Sports|accessdate=11 November 2021|archive-date=11 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211111222431/https://sports.yahoo.com/sweden-shocker-georgia-opens-door-191008817.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Georgia 2–0 Sweden (Nov 11, 2021) Game Analysis |url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/590252 |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=ESPN |language=en |archive-date=11 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111181846/https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/590252 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-11-11 |title=World Cup 2022 qualifying: Sweden's Qatar hopes rocked by defeat in Georgia after Khvicha Kvaratskhelia brace |url=https://www.eurosport.com/football/world-cup-qualification-uefa/2022/world-cup-qualifying-live-georgia-v-sweden_sto8621574/story.shtml |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Eurosport |language=en |archive-date=2 September 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220902204033/https://www.eurosport.com/football/world-cup-qualification-uefa/2022/world-cup-qualifying-live-georgia-v-sweden_sto8621574/story.shtml |url-status=live }}</ref> They continued their improvement by winning the [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League C|2022–23 UEFA Nations League C Group 4]], getting promoted to [[2024-25 UEFA Nations League B]], and qualified for their second-ever [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying play-offs|play-off berth]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Georgian national football team promoted to UEFA Nations League division B after beating North Macedonia |url=https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/3683 |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Agenda.ge |archive-date=11 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111181845/https://agenda.ge/en/news/2022/3683 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-09-25 |title=Georgian national football team promoted to UEFA Nations League division B |url=https://en.trend.az/scaucasus/georgia/3648843.html |access-date=2024-01-11 |website=Trend.Az |language=en |archive-date=11 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111181846/https://en.trend.az/scaucasus/georgia/3648843.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=September 24, 2022 |title=Georgian national football team promoted to UEFA Nations League division B after beating North Macedonia |url=https://azertag.az/en/xeber/georgian_national_football_team_promoted_to_uefa_nations_league_division_b_after_beating_north_macedonia-2306003 |access-date=January 11, 2024 |website=[[Azerbaijan State News Agency]] |archive-date=24 September 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220924112657/https://azertag.az/en/xeber/Georgian_national_football_team_promoted_to_UEFA_Nations_League_division_B_after_beating_North_Macedonia-2306003 |url-status=live }}</ref> Georgia won 2–0 against [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]], earning them a spot in the final of the [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying play-offs]].<ref>{{Cite news |title=Georgia will play with Greece in the final of the European Football Championship playoffs |url=https://newshub.ge/en/news/society/georgia-will-play-with-greece-in-the-final-of-the-european-football-championship-playoffs |work=News Hub}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=March 21, 2024 |title=Georgia one step closer to Euro 2024 after 2–0 win over 10-man Luxembourg |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/georgia-one-step-closer-euro-2024-after-2-0-win-over-10-man-luxembourg-2024-03-21/ |access-date=March 26, 2024 |website=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> They defeated [[Greece national football team|Greece]] 4–2 on penalties after a 0–0 draw in the playoff final, which guaranteed Georgia's qualification for their first ever international tournament at [[UEFA Euro 2024|Euro 2024]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-03-27 |title=Georgia defeated Greece in a penalty shootout and will play at the European Championship for the first time in history |url=https://newshub.ge/en/news/society/georgia-defeated-greece-in-a-penalty-shootout-and-will-play-at-the-european-championship-for-the-first-time-in-history |access-date=2024-03-26 |website=newshub.ge}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Making history: Georgia's national football team will play at Euro 2024, after defeating Greece in playoff final {{!}} AGENDA.GE |url=https://agenda.ge/en/news/2024/38430 |access-date=2024-03-28 |website=agenda.ge |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-03-26 |title=Georgia and star Kvaratskhelia head to Euro 2024 after beating Greece in penalty shootout |url=https://apnews.com/article/euro-2024-playoffs-score-georgia-ukraine-poland-404514c4cf22795004bd975a07e3f888 |access-date=2024-03-26 |website=AP News |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-03-26 |title=Georgia qualify for first European Championships after penalty shootout win over Greece |url=https://www.independent.ie/sport/soccer/international-soccer/georgia-qualify-for-first-european-championships-after-penalty-shootout-win-over-greece/a715665772.html |access-date=2024-03-26 |website=Irish Independent |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Georgia create history by reaching Euro 2024 |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/68603222 |access-date=2024-03-26 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=March 26, 2024 |title=Georgia reach Euro 2024 with Greece shootout win to make history |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/georgia-qualify-euro-2024-after-penalty-shootout-win-over-greece-2024-03-26/ |access-date=March 26, 2024 |website=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> 

During Euro 2024, Georgia were placed in Group F; they subsequently lost 3–1 in the match against [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]], where [[Georges Mikautadze]] scored the first-ever goal in Georgia's history in the Euros, and drew 1–1 against the [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]]. Their 2–0 victory against [[Portugal national football team|Portugal]] was considered one of the biggest upsets in European Championship history,<ref>{{cite web |title=Georgia 2-0 Portugal (Jun 26, 2024) Game Analysis |url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/690607 |website=[[ESPN]] |access-date=26 June 2024}}</ref> and was enough to propel Georgia to the round of 16 in their first international tournament appearance. Georgia subsequently played eventual [[UEFA Euro 2024 Final|Champions]] [[Spain national football team|Spain]] in the Round of 16, and lost by a score of 4–1.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Spain beats Georgia 4-1 to reach Euro 2024 quarterfinals. It next plays host Germany |url=https://www.foxsports.com/articles/soccer/spain-beats-georgia-41-to-reach-euro-2024-quarterfinals-it-next-plays-host-germany |access-date=2024-06-30 |website=FOX Sports |language=en-US}}</ref> [[Georges Mikautadze]] finished the Euro 2024 campaign as the joint-[[UEFA European Football Championship|UEFA European Football Championship Top Goalscorer]].

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Georgia national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were named in the squad for [[Exhibition game|friendly]] matches against [[Romania national football team|Romania]] and [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]] on 2 and 5 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://nakrebi.ge/ka/news/10742-shemadgenloba-ivnisis-amkhanaguri-matchebistvis | title=შემადგენლობა ივნისის ამხანაგური მატჩებისთვის}}</ref>
On May 27, [[Georges Mikautadze]] withdrew from the squad due to injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://nakrebi.ge/ka/team/men-seniors/news/10755-giorgi-mikautadze-nakrebs-amkhanagur-matchebshi|title=გიორგი მიქაუტაძე ნაკრებს ამხანაგურ მატჩებში ვერ დაეხმარება|website=nakrebi.ge}}</ref> On May 31, [[Zuriko Davitashvili]] also withdrew from the squad due to injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://nakrebi.ge/ka/news/10770-sakartvelo-rumineti-1-ivnisis-ganrigi|title=საქართველო - რუმინეთი &#124; 1 ივნისის განრიგი|website=nakrebi.ge}}</ref>
<br>''Caps and goals correct as of 5 June 2026 after the match against [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Giorgi Mamardashvili]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|9|29|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=0|club=[[Liverpool F.C.|Liverpool]]|clubnat=ENG}}

{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Ilia Beriashvili]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|7|9|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[MTK Budapest FC|MTK Budapest]]|clubnat=HUN}}

{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=DF|name=[[Saba Kharebashvili]]|age={{birth date and age|2008|9|3|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[İstanbul Başakşehir F.K.|İstanbul Başakşehir]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Lasha Dvali]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|5|14|df=y}}|caps=48|goals=1||club=[[FC CSKA 1948 Sofia|CSKA 1948]]|clubnat=BUL}}

{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Giorgi Kochorashvili]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|6|29|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=4|club=[[Sporting CP]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=MF|name=[[Saba Lobzhanidze]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|18|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=4|club=[[Atlanta United FC|Atlanta United]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Otar Kiteishvili]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|3|26|df=y}}|caps=54|goals=4|club=[[SK Sturm Graz|Sturm Graz]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Anzor Mekvabishvili]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|6|5|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=0|club=[[CS Universitatea Craiova|Universitatea Craiova]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Gizo Mamageishvili]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|1|15|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[SK Sturm Graz|Sturm Graz]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=MF|name=[[Irakli Yegoian]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|3|19|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Excelsior Rotterdam|Excelsior]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Nika Gagnidze]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|3|20|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[FC Kolos Kovalivka|Kolos Kovalivka]]|clubnat=UKR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Giorgi Chakvetadze]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|29|df=y}}|caps=39|goals=10|club=[[Udinese Calcio|Udinese]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Khvicha Kvaratskhelia]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|2|12|df=y}}|caps=50|goals=23|club=[[Paris Saint-Germain FC|Paris Saint-Germain]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=FW|name=[[Budu Zivzivadze]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|3|10|df=y}}|caps=43|goals=8|club=[[1. FC Heidenheim]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Giorgi Kvilitaia]]|age={{birth date and age|1993|10|1|df=y}}|caps=44|goals=7|club=[[Holstein Kiel]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=FW|name=[[Giorgi Kvernadze]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|2|7|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Frosinone Calcio|Frosinone]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have not been called up for the upcoming matches but have been called up for the team in the last 12 months.

{{nat fs break}}
*<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to injury
*<sup>RET</sup> Player retired from the national team
{{nat fs end}}

==Individual records==
{{updated|5 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Georgia – Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/geor-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=6 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221206151336/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/geor-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Georgia.''

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=15|
!colspan=7|[[FIFA World Cup qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Position
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|Italy|1946}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''{{efn|A total of 14 Georgian players were included in the squads for five of the Soviet Union's seven appearances at the [[FIFA World Cup]]: [[Slava Metreveli]]  ([[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]], [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]], [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Anzor Kavazashvili]] ([[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]], [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Murtaz Khurtsilava]] ([[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]], [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Aleksandr Chivadze]] ([[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]], [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]), [[Givi Chokheli]]  ([[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]), [[Sergey Kotrikadze]] ([[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]), [[Mikhail Meskhi]] ([[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]), [[Georgi Sichinava]] ([[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]), [[Kakhi Asatiani]] ([[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Revaz Dzodzuashvili]] ([[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Givi Nodia]] ([[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]), [[Vitaly Daraselia]] ([[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]), [[Tengiz Sulakvelidze]] ([[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]) and [[Ramaz Shengelia]] ([[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]). There were no Georgian players in the squads of [[1958 FIFA World Cup squads#Soviet Union|1958]] and [[1990 FIFA World Cup squads#Soviet Union|1990]].}}
|colspan=7|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''FIFA member from 1992. Not admitted to the tournament.''{{efn|name=omission|FIFA adopted a decision not to allow the national teams of those former Soviet republics that did not participate in the qualification draw on 8 December 1991 to participate in the 1994 FIFA World Cup.<ref name=kmcrossing>[http://www.kopanyi-myach.info/index.aspx?page=history11 At the crossing (На переправе)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200814063404/http://www.kopanyi-myach.info/index.aspx?page=history11 |date=14 August 2020 }}. Kopanyi myach.</ref> A proposition by Ukraine, supported by Georgia and Armenia, to arrange a separate tournament for all successor states of the Soviet Union was blocked by Russia.<ref name=hwta>{{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305103050/http://fanat.ua/national/02.html We hacked window to America (Прорубили окно в Америку)]}}. Komanda newspaper (by Fanat)</ref>}}
|colspan=7|''Not admitted to the tournament''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|8||3||1||4||7||9||4/5
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|8||3||1||4||12||12||3/5
|-

|-

|}

==Statistics==

!Goals for
!Goals Against

|}

==Honours==
===Friendly===
* '''Trans-Caucasian Championship'''
** '''Champions (3)''': 1928, 1934, 1935
*'''[[Malta International Football Tournament]]'''
** '''Champions (1)''': 1998

*[[Georgia national under-19 football team]]
